# Product Requirements Document
# Generated by req-engine from raw input (Asset Module hierarchy & naming, Apr 2026)
# ---

## 1. Project Overview

The SPM ONE mock asset hierarchy in the Asset Module is being reorganized from a flat unit under one plant to three distinct process units at Site 2000, with renamed equipment and new placeholder equipment rows. Existing functional content for the three primary equipment items (former Coke Drum, HCU, and SMR Unit A under Unit CFR.101) must be preserved: same equipment IDs, same dashboard tab sets, same Workspace seed dashboards and widgets—only display names and parent unit membership change. Site Y and Unit TFR.40 are removed from the mock.

## 2. Goals & Success Criteria

1. The sidebar Asset tree shows exactly one site "Site 2000" (stable id `site-x`) with three children units and the listed equipment under each, matching the target hierarchy.
2. Navigating to Coker 01, HCU 01, or SMR Pigtails (ids `equipment-a`, `equipment-b`, `equipment-c`) preserves all existing tabs, Equipment Home behavior, Workspace published dashboards, and What-If scenarios as today—verified by id, not by old display strings.
3. Placeholder equipment (Coker 02, Coker Furnace, HCU 02, SMR Catalyst Tubes) appear as plain tree nodes; clicking them does not change view or route.
4. No user-facing reference to "Site Y", "Unit TFR.40", "Unit CFR.101" as the active mock labels remains in primary UI or mock data tables.
5. Ontology and PROJECT documentation describe the Site → Unit → Equipment model and the Apr 2026 naming convention.

## 3. Target Users

- **Primary user:** Integrity / process engineer — navigates the asset tree and opens equipment with full content.
- **Secondary user:** Product owner / developer — relies on ontology and mock ids for consistent implementation.

## 4. Functional Requirements

FR-01: The system shall display the site name "Site 2000" for the existing site id `site-x`.

FR-02: The system shall remove the mock site `site-y` and all dependent navigation and data rows that referenced only Site Y.

FR-03: The system shall present three units under Site 2000: "Unit 2006 - DCU", "Unit 2007 - HCU", "Unit 2008 - Hydrogen Unit", with stable ids `unit-2006-dcu`, `unit-2007-hcu`, `unit-2008-h2`.

FR-04: The system shall show equipment "Coker 01" (id `equipment-a") under Unit 2006 - DCU with the same `tabs` array as the former Coke Drum equipment.

FR-05: The system shall show equipment "HCU 01" (id `equipment-b`) under Unit 2007 - HCU with the same `tabs` array as the former HCU equipment.

FR-06: The system shall show equipment "SMR Pigtails" (id `equipment-c`) under Unit 2008 - Hydrogen Unit with the same `tabs` array as the former SMR Unit A equipment.

FR-07: The system shall add placeholder equipment "Coker 02" and "Coker Furnace" under Unit 2006 - DCU with `isPlaceholder: true` and empty or non-navigating behavior on click in the asset menu.

FR-08: The system shall add placeholder equipment "HCU 02" under Unit 2007 - HCU with `isPlaceholder: true` and non-navigating behavior on click in the asset menu.

FR-09: The system shall add placeholder equipment "SMR Catalyst Tubes" under Unit 2008 - Hydrogen Unit with `isPlaceholder: true` and non-navigating behavior on click in the asset menu.

FR-10: The system shall update all mock copy (documents, change log, data sync, What-If labels, AI snippets, deprecated dashboardCards fallbacks) to use the new site and equipment display names where those strings are user-visible.

FR-11: The system shall map `UserDocument.plantId` and similar fields to the new unit ids when documents are scoped to a unit.

FR-12: The system shall update Site Overview map regions and filter dropdowns to list the three units instead of former Plant 1 / Plant 2 and Unit TFR.40.

## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

NFR-01: Compatibility — Stable equipment ids `equipment-a`, `equipment-b`, `equipment-c` shall not change; Workspace `equipmentId` on existing seeded dashboards shall remain valid without re-keying.

NFR-02: Consistency — The domain ontology file shall document the unit tier and naming; PROJECT.md shall align with the ontology summary.

NFR-03: Discoverability — A single helper (or equivalent) shall resolve which unit contains a given `equipmentId` for navigation path defaults.

## 6. Out of Scope

- Changing widget layouts or dashboard business logic for non-name reasons.
- Backend API or real persistence.
- New functional behavior for placeholder equipment beyond non-clickable menu rows.

## 7. Constraints

- Must not remove or replace seeded dashboard widget grids or tab names for the three primary equipment items except renaming user-visible strings that refer to old asset names.
- Must delete only Site Y and Unit TFR.40 mock data, not unrelated demo rows (e.g. "Pipe a") unless they depended on removed sites.

## 8. Assumptions Made

- [ ] "Plant" in `NavigationPath` and store (`expandedPlants`, etc.) continues to hold unit ids; renaming TypeScript field names to `unit` is optional follow-up — verify for backend alignment.
- [ ] Placeholder equipment does not require Workspace dashboards or What-If scenarios in v1.
- [ ] "Plain nodes" in the menu means no navigation; optional muted styling is acceptable if consistent with existing sidebar patterns.

## 9. Open Questions

- [x] Rename `plants` to `units` in `lib/data.ts` — **done** (Apr 28 2026); all consumers updated.

## 10. Suggested Task Breakdown Hints

- Ontology & PROJECT documentation
- `lib/data.ts` hierarchy, types, traversal helpers, mock tables, What-If, documents
- Workspace seed string updates (`eqByName` / display titles)
- Sidebar: placeholder click suppression
- Site Overview / Plant Overview: unit filters, path resolution, map
- `lib/workspace-data.ts`, documents, what-if shared path helper
- Remaining string sweep (home constants, AI features, create-dashboard placeholder)
